lastFailureSignature: opencli|requires-interactive-input|SponsorLink syncs your sponsorship(s) to your local machine, enabling package authors to provide selective functionality to sponsors. ── How it works ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── GitHub users and organizations using 8;id=1779485178;https://github.com/sponsors/GitHub Sponsors8;; (a.k.a. sponsorables) can opt-in to SponsorLink by providing 8;id=1509094598;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/spec#sponsorable-manifesta standard JWT manifest8;; at 8;id=376130190;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwthttps://github.com/8;;8;id=1954452282;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwt[user/org]8;;8;id=1311475195;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwt/.github/sponsorlink.jwt8;;. This is the same location used for other 8;id=1296906571;https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/creating-a-default-community-health-filecommunity health files8;;. When you synchronize your sponsorship with a given account, the following steps take place: 👉 read sponsorable JWT manifest from https://github.com/[user/org] /.github/sponsorlink.jwt (8;id=951652582;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwtexample8;;) 👉 read from the manifest the issuer URI, public key and the sponsorable's GitHub OAuth client (app) ID 👉 request GitHub OAuth app authorization via the default browser to get a limited acccess token 👀 you should read the permissions requested by the sponsorable's GitHub app carefully ✅ you can revoke these permissions at any time at 8;id=1427984445;https://github.com/settings/applicationshttps://github.com/settings/applications8;; 👉 issue a GET request with that limited token to [issuer URI]/me and get a sponsor JWT manifest 👀 the backend will only have access to the information you authorized 👉 verify and save sponsor JWT manifest locally for later offline access and validation by libraries and tools 👀 Sponsor manifests are cached under ~/.sponsorlink ( %userprofile%\.sponsorlink on Windows). You can easily inspect the JWT of both the sponsorable account and your sponsor manifest using 8;id=482739301;https://jwt.io/https://jwt.io/8;;, for example. This manifest needs to be sync'ed once a month, which is the only point at which each SponsorLink backend computes, signs your sponsorship manifest and sets a new expiration date. You can also enable 8;id=1687966442;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/github#auto-syncautomatic synchronization8;;. Learn more at 8;id=1691915669;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/8;;. ── Privacy policy ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── SponsorLink is build by developers for developers. As such, we don't have an attorney or a big corporation backing this. If you're running this tool, it's likely because you are currently sponsoring or considering sponsoring the developer of a package you enjoy. The short story is: 👉 sponsorship manifests are purely local and offline ✅ no info persisted in the cloud 👉 the backend uses a limited GitHub OAuth app token ✅ only email(s) and organization membership determine sponsorships 👉 you can be a direct or indirect supporter/sponsor 💜 implicit sponsorship via an organization or project contributions Code for the backend and this app are 8;id=1839789421;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/open source8;;. Please read the 8;id=774805122;https://github.com/devlooped/SponsorLink/blob/main/privacy.mdfull 8;; 8;id=511245638;https://github.com/devlooped/SponsorLink/blob/main/privacy.mdprivacy policy8;; to learn more. ── Telemetry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Each sponsor issuer backend may collect anonymous usage data telemetry to improve your experience. To send your preference to not get such telemetry collected, you can set a SPONSOR_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT environment variable to '1' or 'true' using your favorite shell. You can read more about telemetry 8;id=1365716429;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/github.html#telemetryin the docs8;;. ? Do you accept the above usage terms? [y/n] (y): Error: Failed to read input in non-interactive mode.
lastFailureMessage:
SponsorLink syncs your sponsorship(s) to your local machine, enabling         
  package authors to provide selective functionality to sponsors.               
                                                                                
  ── How it works ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  GitHub users and organizations using 8;id=1779485178;https://github.com/sponsors/GitHub Sponsors8;; (a.k.a. sponsorables)    
  can opt-in to SponsorLink by providing 8;id=1509094598;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/spec#sponsorable-manifesta standard JWT manifest8;; at             
  8;id=376130190;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwthttps://github.com/8;;8;id=1954452282;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwt[user/org]8;;8;id=1311475195;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwt/.github/sponsorlink.jwt8;;. This is the same       
  location used for other 8;id=1296906571;https://docs.github.com/en/communities/setting-up-your-project-for-healthy-contributions/creating-a-default-community-health-filecommunity health files8;;.                               
                                                                                
  When you synchronize your sponsorship with a given account, the following     
  steps take place:                                                             
                                                                                
  👉 read sponsorable JWT manifest from https://github.com/[user/org]           
  /.github/sponsorlink.jwt (8;id=951652582;https://github.com/devlooped/.github/blob/main/sponsorlink.jwtexample8;;)                                            
  👉 read from the manifest the issuer URI, public key and the sponsorable's    
  GitHub OAuth client (app) ID                                                  
  👉 request GitHub OAuth app authorization via the default browser to get a    
  limited acccess token                                                         
     👀 you should read the permissions requested by the sponsorable's GitHub   
  app carefully                                                                 
     ✅ you can revoke these permissions at any time at                         
  8;id=1427984445;https://github.com/settings/applicationshttps://github.com/settings/applications8;;                                      
  👉 issue a GET request with that limited token to [issuer URI]/me and get a   
  sponsor JWT manifest                                                          
     👀 the backend will only have access to the information you authorized     
  👉 verify and save sponsor JWT manifest locally for later offline access and  
  validation by libraries and tools                                             
    👀 Sponsor manifests are cached under ~/.sponsorlink (                      
  %userprofile%\.sponsorlink on Windows).                                       
                                                                                
  You can easily inspect the JWT of both the sponsorable account and your       
  sponsor manifest using 8;id=482739301;https://jwt.io/https://jwt.io/8;;, for example.                          
                                                                                
  This manifest needs to be sync'ed once a month, which is the only point at    
  which each SponsorLink backend computes, signs your sponsorship manifest and  
  sets a new expiration date. You can also enable 8;id=1687966442;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/github#auto-syncautomatic synchronization8;;.    
  Learn more at 8;id=1691915669;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/8;;.                         
                                                                                
  ── Privacy policy ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  SponsorLink is build by developers for developers. As such, we don't have an  
  attorney or a big corporation backing this. If you're running this tool,      
  it's likely because you are currently sponsoring or considering sponsoring    
  the developer of a package you enjoy.                                         
                                                                                
  The short story is:                                                           
  👉 sponsorship manifests are purely local and offline ✅ no info persisted    
  in the cloud                                                                  
  👉 the backend uses a limited GitHub OAuth app token  ✅ only email(s) and    
  organization membership determine sponsorships                                
  👉 you can be a direct or indirect supporter/sponsor  💜 implicit             
  sponsorship via an organization or project contributions                      
                                                                                
  Code for the backend and this app are 8;id=1839789421;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/open source8;;. Please read the 8;id=774805122;https://github.com/devlooped/SponsorLink/blob/main/privacy.mdfull 8;;      
  8;id=511245638;https://github.com/devlooped/SponsorLink/blob/main/privacy.mdprivacy policy8;; to learn more.                                                 
                                                                                
  ── Telemetry ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  
  Each sponsor issuer backend may collect anonymous usage data telemetry to     
  improve your experience. To send your preference to not get such telemetry    
  collected, you can set a SPONSOR_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT environment variable    
  to '1' or 'true' using your favorite shell. You can read more about           
  telemetry 8;id=1365716429;https://www.devlooped.com/SponsorLink/github.html#telemetryin the docs8;;.                                                        
? Do you accept the above usage terms? [y/n] (y): Error: Failed to read input in non-interactive mode.
